summarization.a: In-plane 2θχ XRD comparing 200cLi-MnO₂-600 (annealed) to 200cLi-MnO₂ (unannealed), showing that annealing produces clear LiMn₂O₄ spinel reflections at (111), (131), (222), (440), and (351). An impurity peak (asterisk) is visible in the annealed sample.
summarization.b: Out-of-plane θ-2θ XRD of the same samples, revealing that annealing at 600°C causes β-MnO₂ reflections (100), (200) to reappear alongside the lithiated LiMn₂O₄ phase with (111) and (311) peaks.
summarization.c: In-plane 2θχ XRD comparing 300cLi-MnO₂-600 (annealed) to 300cLi-MnO₂ (unannealed), demonstrating improved crystallinity and sharper LiMn₂O₄ spinel reflections after annealing.
summarization.d: Out-of-plane θ-2θ XRD of the same samples, showing LiMn₂O₄ (111) and (311) reflections with an impurity phase Li₂₋ₓMnO₂ marked by an asterisk at ~32.6°.

d.question: What does the asterisk (*) in panels (a) and (d) indicate?
d.answer: An impurity reflection from the Li₂₋ᵤMnO₂ phase, identified by comparison with standard XRD reference patterns

c.question: Why does β-MnO₂ reappear after annealing 200cLi-MnO₂ but not 300cLi-MnO₂?
c.answer: The 200cLi sample has insufficient lithium for complete conversion; annealing causes Li redistribution, leaving some regions lithium-deficient and reverting to MnO₂.

a.question: What effect does annealing at 600°C have on the crystal structure?
a.answer: Annealing enables lithium to overcome diffusion barriers, completing the transformation to stoichiometric cubic LiMn₂O₄ with sharper, better-indexed reflections. However, it also causes some phase separation, particularly in 200cLi-MnO₂-600 where β-MnO₂ reappears.

a.question: Do the annealed samples match the stoichiometric cubic LiMn₂O₄ reference better than unannealed ones?
a.answer: Yes. The in-plane reflections shift to angles matching the stoichiometric LiMn₂O₄ reference pattern, and additional reflections like (111), (222), and (351) become clearly visible.
